message:
fix(ffs-core): pass actual middle-segment length to btrfs_push_prealloc_rewrite_segment (bd-qk5kv)

Follow-on to e166fbb. The newly-added prealloc-coalescing path passed
`overlap_end - key.offset` as the middle-segment length, but that
expression is the offset of the segment's *end* relative to the
extent header, not the segment's length. The segment actually starts
at `overlap_start`, so the correct length is `overlap_end - overlap_start`.

Concretely:
  - middle segment logical range: [overlap_start, overlap_end)
  - length wanted by push_prealloc_rewrite_segment: overlap_end - overlap_start

The old expression silently inflated the length by `(overlap_start
- key.offset)` whenever the rewrite did not start at the head of the
covering extent — i.e. exactly the case bd-qk5kv exists to cover.
For an extent at key.offset = 0 with a hole-then-zero range from
[16K, 32K), the old length was 32K instead of 16K, walking the
prealloc emission off the end of the actual zero-range slice and
masking the very fragmentation problem the prealloc-coalescing helper
was added to detect.

Single-character fix; no public API change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
